label: The Glassvein Hollow
description: The Glassvein Hollow opens as a low, iron-stained chamber cut through Blackchisel Mountain’s vitrified seams, its walls glazed in dark green glass where ancient heat ran through the rock. Broken boundary steles lie stacked as crude revetments along the floor, their older carvings chiseled over with later charter seals. Narrow fissures in the ceiling admit a constant thread of wind that carries grit and sets the glassy surfaces ringing with a thin, persistent resonance. Toolmarks and shallow extraction pits remain visible along the brightest bands, preserving the cave’s history as a regulated source of prized stone.
